mnemonics.title: Memory Hierarchy Levels
mnemonics.content: CRAMS: Cache, Registers, Auxiliary, Main, Secondary - arranged by decreasing speed

mnemonics.title: Cache Mapping Types
mnemonics.content: DAFU: Direct (fixed line), Fully (any line), Set (hybrid) - Associative mapping types

mnemonics.title: Write Policies
mnemonics.content: WISP: Write-In Simultaneous (Through), Save-first (Back) - two main write policies

acronyms.usage: Cache replacement policy that evicts least recently accessed block
Cache replacement policy that evicts oldest block
Cache replacement policy that evicts least accessed block
Cache for virtual to physical address translation
Primary volatile memory for programs and data
Fast, expensive memory used for cache (6 transistors per bit)
Main memory (1 transistor + capacitor per bit, needs refresh)
Hardware used for associative/fully-associative cache lookup

visualTips.visualization: Draw inverted pyramid: Registers (top, smallest), Cache, Main Memory, Secondary Storage (bottom, largest). Label with speed decreasing upward to downward.
Divide address into Tag | Index | Offset. Show how each field determines cache line location and data selection.
Decision diamond: CPU requests → Check cache → Hit? → Yes (return data) / No (fetch from memory, update cache, return)
Side-by-side: Write-Through (both update simultaneously) vs Write-Back (cache first, memory on eviction)

analogies.analogy: Library = Main Memory, Desk = Cache, Brain = Registers. When studying, keep frequently used books at desk (cache), reference library only when needed (miss).
Walk-in freezer = Main memory, Fridge = Cache, Counter = Registers. Chefs grab ingredients from nearest location first, reducing trips to freezer.
Filing cabinet = Main memory, Desk drawer = Cache, In-use folder = Registers. Active work stays at desk, completed work goes to cabinet.
